title: Understanding the Problem
text: To find the displacement of a particle moving with constant acceleration, we can use the kinematic equation: s $$=$$ ut + $$0.5{at}^2$$, where s is the displacement, u is the initial velocity, a is the acceleration, and $$t$$ is the time.

title: Identify the Given Values
text: From the problem, we know the initial velocity (u) is $$30$$ $$\frac{m}{s}$$, the acceleration (a) is $$30$$ $$\frac{m}{s^2}$$, and the time (t) is $$5$$ seconds.

title: Substitute the Values into the Equation
text: Substitute the known values into the equation: s $$=$$ $$30\times5$$ + $$0.5\times30 5^2$$.

title: Calculate the First Term
text: Calculate the first term of the equation: $$30\times5$$.

title: Calculate the Second Term
text: Calculate the second term of the equation: $$0.5\times30 5^2$$.

title: Add the Two Terms Together
text: Add the results of the two terms to find the displacement: $$150+375$$.
